# bb — Bitbucket Cloud CLI

`bb` is a command-line tool for the Bitbucket Cloud REST API v2.0.
It is designed to be used by AI agents, but humans can use it too!

## Installation

```bash
# macOS / Linux via Homebrew
brew tap payfacto/tap
brew install payfacto/tap/bb

# Pre-built binaries (Linux/macOS/Windows, amd64+arm64)
# Download an archive from https://github.com/payfacto/bb/releases
# and place the `bb` binary on your PATH.

# Go developers
go install github.com/payfacto/bb@latest

# Or build locally
go build -o bb .
```

## Authentication

### Option A — OAuth 2.0 (recommended)

```bash
bb auth login    # browser-based OAuth flow, token stored in OS keyring
bb auth status   # show current auth state
bb auth logout   # remove stored credentials
bb auth token    # print raw token for scripts
```

### Option B — API Token *(replaces App Passwords)*

App Passwords are being phased out by Atlassian. Use an API token or scoped token instead.
See: https://support.atlassian.com/bitbucket-cloud/docs/api-tokens/

```bash
bb setup         # interactive wizard, stores API token in OS keyring
```

### Option C — Environment variables (CI/CD)

```bash
export BITBUCKET_USER=myusername
export BITBUCKET_TOKEN=myapitoken
bb pr list -w myws -r myrepo
```

### Configuration file

`~/.bbcloud.yaml` stores workspace, repo, username, and auth type. Tokens are never written to disk — they live in the OS keyring or environment variables.

## Global flags

All commands accept:
- `-w`, `--workspace SLUG` — Bitbucket workspace slug
- `-r`, `--repo SLUG` — repository slug
- `-f`, `--format json|text` — output format (default: json; piped stdout forces json)
- `--config PATH` — path to config file
- `--describe` — emit a JSON capability manifest (commands, flags, schemas) and exit (no auth required)

## Capability manifest (`--describe`)

`bb --describe` prints a JSON document describing every command, its flags, its action class (`read | write | destructive`), an example invocation, the output Go type name, and an auto-generated JSON Schema for both output and stdin input (when supported). Use this to discover the surface programmatically instead of parsing `--help`. The manifest is sorted by command path and stable across runs.

The top-level `manifest_schema_version` field (currently `"1"`) is bumped independently of the CLI version when the manifest shape changes. Branch on it, not on the CLI's semver. Stdin entries carry a `behavior` enum (`"replaces_flags"` today) describing how piped JSON interacts with flags.

## Error format

On failure, `bb` writes a single JSON object to **stderr** and exits non-zero. stdout is never mixed with errors. The shape is:

```json
{"error": {"code": "not_found", "message": "HTTP 404: ...", "details": {"http_status": 404, "response_body": "..."}}}
```

Codes are a fixed enum: `config_missing`, `auth_failed`, `not_found`, `validation_failed`, `conflict`, `rate_limited`, `api_error`, `internal_error`.

For API errors, `details.response_body` is **redacted by default** (only `details.response_body_redacted: true` appears) because Bitbucket 4xx responses sometimes echo back request fragments. Set `BB_DEBUG=1` to include the raw response body for local troubleshooting. For required-flag failures, `details.missing_flags` is a `[]string` of the flags Cobra is complaining about.

## Stdin input

Create and update commands accept a JSON body on stdin as an alternative to flags. When stdin is piped and non-empty, it replaces all flag values (no merging — `stdin.behavior` enum is `"replaces_flags"`). Stdin input is capped at 1 MiB; oversized input is rejected as `validation_failed`. The manifest's `stdin.schema` field shows the expected shape. Examples:

```bash
echo '{"title":"fix","source":{"branch":{"name":"feat/x"}},"destination":{"branch":{"name":"main"}}}' | bb pr create
echo '{"key":"API_KEY","value":"secret","secured":true}' | bb pipeline-var create
```

Stdin-capable commands: `pr create`, `pr comment add`, `branch create`, `tag create`, `repo create`, `repo update`, `repo fork`, `issue create`, `webhook create`, `deploy-key add`, `pipeline-var create`, `restriction create`.

## Commands

### Pull Requests

```
bb pr list [-s OPEN|MERGED|DECLINED|SUPERSEDED] [--source-branch BRANCH] [--sort FIELD]
bb pr get -p ID
bb pr create -T "..." --from-branch BRANCH --to-branch BRANCH [-d "..."] [--close-source-branch] [--draft]
bb pr diff -p ID
bb pr approve -p ID
bb pr merge -p ID [--strategy merge_commit|squash|fast_forward]
bb pr decline -p ID
bb pr activity -p ID
bb pr statuses -p ID
bb pr add-reviewer -p ID --account-id ACCOUNT_ID
```

### PR Comments

```
bb pr comment list -p ID
bb pr comment get -p ID -c COMMENT_ID
bb pr comment add -p ID -t "..."
bb pr comment reply -p ID -c COMMENT_ID -t "..."
```

### PR Tasks

```
bb pr task list -p ID
bb pr task complete -p ID --task-id ID
bb pr task reopen -p ID --task-id ID
```

### Pipelines

```
bb pipeline list [--sort FIELD]
bb pipeline get -u UUID
bb pipeline trigger -b BRANCH
bb pipeline stop -u UUID
bb pipeline steps -u UUID
bb pipeline log -u UUID --step-uuid UUID
```

### Branches

```
bb branch list [--sort FIELD]
bb branch create -n BRANCH --from COMMIT_OR_BRANCH
bb branch delete -n BRANCH
```

### Tags

```
bb tag list [--sort FIELD]
bb tag create -n TAG --from COMMIT
bb tag delete -n TAG
```

### Commits

```
bb commit list -b BRANCH [--sort FIELD]
bb commit get -x HASH
bb file get --ref REF --path PATH
```

### Repositories

```
bb repo list [--sort FIELD]
bb repo get SLUG
bb repo update SLUG [--description "..."] [--default-branch BRANCH]
bb repo delete SLUG
bb repo create SLUG [--name "..."] [--description "..."] [--private] [--project KEY]
bb repo fork SLUG [--name "..."] [--workspace SLUG]
```

### Issues

```
bb issue list [--sort FIELD]
bb issue get -i ID
bb issue create -T "..." [-d "..."] [-k bug|enhancement|proposal|task] [--priority trivial|minor|major|critical|blocker]
bb issue close -i ID
bb issue reopen -i ID
```

### Pipeline Variables

```
bb pipeline-var list
bb pipeline-var create -k KEY -v VALUE [--secured]
bb pipeline-var delete --uuid UUID
```

### Deployments & Environments

```
bb deployment list
bb env list
```

### Workspaces

```
bb workspace list
```

### Members & Users

```
bb member list
bb user me
```

### Webhooks

```
bb webhook list
bb webhook create --url URL --events EVENT,EVENT [--description "..."] [--active]
bb webhook delete --uuid UUID
```

### Deploy Keys

```
bb deploy-key list
bb deploy-key add --label "..." --key "ssh-rsa ..."
bb deploy-key delete -i ID
```

### Branch Restrictions

```
bb restriction list
bb restriction create --kind KIND --pattern PATTERN [--value N]
bb restriction delete -i ID
```

### Downloads

```
bb download list
bb download upload --file PATH
bb download delete --name FILENAME
```

## Output

Default output is newline-terminated JSON on **stdout** (machine-readable).
Pass `-f text` for human-readable styled terminal output.
`bb pr diff` and `bb pipeline log` always output plain text regardless of `-f`.
When stdout is not a TTY, `-f` defaults to `json` regardless of any future default change.
Errors are emitted as JSON on **stderr** — see "Error format" above.

## Notes for AI agents

- Always use `bb <command>` with explicit subcommands. Running bare `bb` launches an interactive TUI meant for humans — agents should never do this.
- All list commands return JSON arrays; single-resource commands return a JSON object.
- IDs are integers for PRs, tasks, issues, deploy keys, and restrictions. UUIDs (with `{}` braces) for pipelines, steps, environments, and webhooks.
- The CLI exits non-zero on errors and writes structured JSON `{"error": {"code", "message", "details"}}` to stderr (see "Error format").
- Use `bb --describe` to enumerate every command, flag, action class, and JSON Schema in a single call.
- `--sort` is available on `pr list`, `pipeline list`, `branch list`, `tag list`, `commit list`, `repo list`, and `issue list`. Other list commands do not accept `--sort` because the underlying Bitbucket endpoint does not — sort their JSON output client-side if you need deterministic ordering.
- Workspace and repo can be omitted from flags if set in `~/.bbcloud.yaml`.
- All long flags have short equivalents: `-p` (pr-id), `-s` (state), `-b` (branch), `-u` (pipeline-uuid), `-c` (comment-id), `-t` (text), `-T` (title), `-d` (description), `-n` (name), `-i` (id), `-k` (kind or key), `-v` (value), `-x` (hash).
- Pipeline variable UUIDs are returned as strings with `{}` braces (e.g. `{abc-123}`); pass the full UUID including braces to `--uuid`.
